title: Improve first time experience with `podman build --platform` argument
body:
### Feature request description

I recently migrated my personal websites from `docker` to `podman` as I personally didn't like the direction that Docker (the company) was pursuing. Keep in mind I'm a complete beginner when it comes to `podman` but I had used `docker` up until a few years ago.

I had a previous build script that invoked `docker build` with the argument `--platform=amd64`. 

After aliasing podman within my shell `alias docker=podman` I re-ran my build script and received the error:

```
Error: invalid platform syntax for "amd64" (use OS/ARCH[/VARIANT][,...])
```

Granted, I haven't rebuilt my site's container in quite some time (at least a few years ago) but this did once work with `docker`. 

I was able to quickly find Podman's [documentation page for the `--platform` argument](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-build.1.html#platform-os-arch-variant) (love that I can direct anchor link to this) and was able to correct my build script to supply the correct `--platform=linux/amd64` argument.

Then I tried to find Docker's documentation for its `--platform` argument. I couldn't find anything [but this](https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/build/) (not useful). So now I'm not sure if this ever worked.

I'm not sure how similar (or not) these APIs are meant to be. If podman is intended to be a drop-in replacement it would be nice to improve this first problem encounter experience. Others are likely to have this problem and may give up on podman. 




### Suggest potential solution

I'm not sure how similar (or not) these APIs are meant to be. If podman is intended to be a drop-in replacement I have the following suggestions:

1. Make podman's `invalid platform syntax ` error message more verbose regarding this problem:
  * Perhaps it could include a link to the [documentation for the flag](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-build.1.html#platform-os-arch-variant)?
  * Could it list concrete examples -- ie `linux/amd64`, etc
1. (if there isn't one already) documentation describing the specific argument differences between podman and docker

### Have you considered any alternatives?

N/A - not reverting back to `docker build`

### Additional context

As a side note: this was the only change I had to make to move my container build from `docker` to `podman`. Great work.

comments.body:
Can you specify the actual podman/docker command you are running, or a reproducer?

Also we need `podman info` output so we know what version of podman and what environment you are running on.

@rhatdan Podman command:

```
podman build \
  --squash \
  --platform=linux/amd64 \
  --tag my-name \
  --file Containerfile \
  .
```

`$ podman info`

@aren55555 In most of the commands usually error message dont point to `man pages` but I have made error a bit cleaner if that helps. https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4757
